label: yes
cot_prefix: Okay, let's see. The question is asking which statement is NOT an advantage of the algorithm described in the passage. First, let me go through the passage again to understand the key points. The monitor watches for deceleration and distance from a light, guessing which cars might break the Highway Code. It can provide two seconds' warning if someone is about to shoot across a crossroads, allowing other drivers to brake. Professor How mentions that future smart cars might use this system to help drivers foresee and avoid accidents. The researchers tested it in Virginia, finding it accurately identified potential violators a couple of seconds before reaching a red light, giving others time to react. Compared to other models, this MIT algorithm has fewer false alarms, which is important for human drivers. The paper is to appear in IEEE Transactions on Intelligent Transportation Systems. Now, the options are: A) It will raise the alarm when the driver breaks the traffic rules. B) It usually produces fewer false alarms. C) It helps drivers avoid potential accidents by judging the threats. D) The monitor of this algorithm can judge the traffic condition from the lights of other vehicles. Let me check each against the passage. The passage says the algorithm can provide a two-second warning if someone is about to cross a crossroads. So, it's raising an alarm when a driver is about to break the rules (run a red light). So A seems to be an advantage. B says it produces fewer false alarms. The passage explicitly states that compared to other efforts, the MIT algorithm brought fewer false alarms. So B is an advantage. C mentions helping avoid accidents by judging threats. The professor says future smart cars may use this to help drivers foresee and avoid accidents. So C is an advantage. D says the monitor can judge traffic condition from the lights of other vehicles. The passage says the monitor watches deceleration and distance from a light. It doesn't mention using the lights of other vehicles. It's about the distance from the light (probably traffic light) and deceleration of the car. The
